top_articles.reason: Significant safety concern with potential widespread impact on air traffic.
top_articles.title: The War In Iran Has Made This Small Spanish Airport Very Popular
top_articles.summary_en: The ongoing war in Iran has disrupted airline operations across the Gulf region, leading to increased traffic at a small rural airport in Spain, which is being utilized by affected airlines. This unexpected popularity highlights the broader impact of geopolitical conflicts on global aviation.
top_articles.summary_zh: 伊朗的战争导致海湾地区的航空运营受到干扰，西班牙一个偏远的小机场因此迎来了更多航班，受到影响的航空公司纷纷使用该机场。这一意外的受欢迎程度突显了地缘政治冲突对全球航空业的广泛影响。

top_articles.reason: Serious threat to aviation safety due to stolen aircraft parts.
top_articles.title: EASA Warns Of Stolen Aircraft Parts Threatening Global Flights
top_articles.summary_en: EASA has reported the theft of over 600 aircraft parts, including critical components from four engine families, after a shipment was illegally rerouted. The agency is warning that these stolen parts could be resold and pose significant safety risks, urging buyers to avoid acquiring them.
top_articles.summary_zh: 欧洲航空安全局（EASA）报告称，超过600个飞机部件被盗，包括来自四个发动机系列的关键组件，此次盗窃发生在一批货物被非法重新路由后。该机构警告这些被盗部件可能被转售，并可能对安全构成重大风险，敦促买家避免购买这些部件。

top_articles.reason: Tragic event honoring pilots, resonates emotionally with readers.
top_articles.title: “Today We Mourn”: Pilots Of Air Canada Flight 8646 Honored As Bodies Return Home
top_articles.summary_en: Hundreds gathered outside Air Canada's Montreal headquarters to honor Captain Antoine Forest and First Officer Mackenzie Gunther, who tragically lost their lives in the crash of Air Canada Flight AC8646 at LaGuardia Airport. Their remains were returned home amid emotional tributes from fellow pilots and crew members, as investigations into the incident continue.
top_articles.summary_zh: 数百人在加拿大航空蒙特利尔总部外聚集，向在拉瓜迪亚机场空难中不幸遇难的安托万·福雷斯特机长和麦肯齐·冈瑟副驾驶致敬。他们的遗体在同事和机组人员的感人致敬中被送回家，事故调查仍在进行中。

top_articles.reason: Impact on airline operations and passenger travel options.
top_articles.title: End Of An Era: Major Airline Abruptly Axes London Heathrow Long-Haul Route
top_articles.summary_en: Saudia is set to suspend its long-haul route from London Heathrow to Dammam, Saudi Arabia, just five months after its relaunch due to low load factors, with flights operating at an average of only 49.5% capacity. The route, which began in November 2025, will officially cease operations on April 18, 2026.
top_articles.summary_zh: 沙特航空将暂停其从伦敦希思罗机场到达曼的长途航线，仅在重新开通五个月后，由于载客率低，航班平均仅为49.5%的容量。该航线于2025年11月开始，将于2026年4月18日正式停止运营。

top_articles.reason: Relevant military aviation insights, appealing to defense enthusiasts.
top_articles.title: 5 Critical Performance Metrics That Distinguish The F-22 Raptor From Modern Stealth Rivals
top_articles.summary_en: The F-22 Raptor remains the leading fifth-generation fighter in 2026, distinguished by its unparalleled low radar cross-section, all-aspect stealth, high operational ceiling, and superior speed and maneuverability. Despite being older than its rivals, it outperforms them in key metrics, making it a dominant force in aerial combat.
top_articles.summary_zh: 到2026年，F-22猛禽仍然是领先的第五代战斗机，其无与伦比的低雷达截面、全方位隐身、高作战高度以及卓越的速度和机动性使其脱颖而出。尽管比竞争对手年长，但在关键指标上超越了它们，使其成为空中战斗中的主导力量。

shownotes_en:
Welcome back to the Aviation News Podcast! I’m your host, Dudu, and today is March 28, 2026. We have some fascinating stories lined up for you, showcasing the ever-evolving world of aviation. Let’s dive right in!

First up, the ongoing war in Iran has had some unexpected consequences for air travel. With airline operations across the Gulf region severely disrupted, a small rural airport in Spain has found itself suddenly popular. This little airport is now being utilized by several airlines affected by the conflict, demonstrating how geopolitical events can ripple through the aviation industry in surprising ways. It’s a reminder of just how interconnected our world is, especially in times of crisis.

Speaking of crises, let’s turn our attention to safety concerns. The European Union Aviation Safety Agency, or EASA, has issued a stark warning regarding the theft of over 600 aircraft parts, including critical components from four different engine families. These parts were illegally rerouted and could potentially end up on the black market, posing significant safety risks for airlines and passengers alike. EASA is urging anyone in the market for aircraft parts to be vigilant and avoid acquiring anything that may have been stolen. It’s a sobering reminder of the importance of maintaining stringent safety standards in aviation.

In more somber news, the aviation community is in mourning as we honor the pilots of Air Canada Flight 8646. Captain Antoine Forest and First Officer Mackenzie Gunther tragically lost their lives in a crash at LaGuardia Airport. Hundreds gathered outside Air Canada’s Montreal headquarters to pay their respects as their remains were returned home. Fellow pilots and crew members shared emotional tributes, reflecting on the deep bonds formed in the skies. Investigations into the incident continue, but today, we remember the dedication and bravery of these two individuals.

On a different note, we’re witnessing the end of an era in long-haul travel. Saudia Airlines has announced the suspension of its London Heathrow to Dammam route, just five months after its relaunch. The route struggled with low load factors, averaging only about 49.5% capacity since its restart in November 2025. Officially ceasing operations on April 18, 2026, this decision underscores the challenges airlines face in maintaining profitable routes in a competitive market.

Finally, let’s shift gears to military aviation. The F-22 Raptor continues to reign supreme as the leading fifth-generation fighter in 2026. Despite being older than its modern rivals, it stands out with its unmatched low radar cross-section, all-aspect stealth capabilities, high operational ceiling, and exceptional speed and maneuverability. These critical performance metrics ensure that the F-22 remains a dominant force in aerial combat, proving that age doesn’t always determine capability.

That wraps up today’s episode of the Aviation News Podcast. Thank you for joining me, Dudu, as we explored these intriguing stories from the aviation world. Until next time, safe travels and clear skies!
shownotes_zh:
欢迎回到航空新闻播客！我是你的主持人Dudu，今天是2026年3月28日。我们为您准备了一些引人入胜的故事，展示航空界的不断演变。让我们直接开始吧！

首先，伊朗的战争对航空旅行产生了一些意想不到的影响。由于海湾地区的航空公司运营受到严重干扰，西班牙的一家小型农村机场突然变得非常受欢迎。这家小机场现在被几家受冲突影响的航空公司利用，展示了地缘政治事件如何以意想不到的方式波及航空业。这提醒我们，在危机时期，我们的世界是多么紧密相连。

说到危机，我们来关注一下安全问题。欧洲航空安全局（EASA）发出了关于超过600个飞机零部件被盗的严峻警告，包括来自四种不同发动机系列的关键部件。这些零部件被非法重新路由，可能会在黑市上流通，给航空公司和乘客带来重大安全风险。EASA敦促任何在寻找飞机零部件的人保持警惕，避免购买任何可能被盗的物品。这是一个令人警醒的提醒，强调了在航空领域保持严格安全标准的重要性。

在更沉重的消息中，航空界正在悼念Air Canada 8646航班的飞行员。机长Antoine Forest和副驾驶Mackenzie Gunther在拉瓜迪亚机场的坠毁中不幸丧生。数百人在Air Canada位于蒙特利尔的总部外聚集，向他们致以敬意，随着他们的遗体回家，同行的飞行员和机组人员分享了情感丰沛的致敬，反映出在空中形成的深厚纽带。事故调查仍在进行中，但今天，我们铭记这两位个体的奉献和勇气。

另一方面，我们在长途旅行中见证了一个时代的结束。沙特阿拉伯航空宣布暂停从伦敦希思罗到达曼的航线，仅在重新启动五个月后。这条航线由于低载客率而苦苦挣扎，自2025年11月重新启动以来，平均仅有49.5%的载客率。该航线将于2026年4月18日正式停止运营，这一决定凸显了航空公司在竞争激烈的市场中维持盈利航线所面临的挑战。

最后，让我们转向军事航空。F-22猛禽战斗机在2026年继续作为领先的第五代战斗机。尽管比现代竞争对手年长，但它以无与伦比的低雷达截面、全方位隐身能力、高作战高度以及卓越的速度和机动性脱颖而出。这些关键的性能指标确保F-22在空中作战中保持主导地位，证明年龄并不总是决定能力。

今天的航空新闻播客就到这里。感谢您与我Dudu一起探索这些航空界的引人入胜的故事。下次见，祝您旅途顺利，天空晴朗！
